leadTime.sentenceTemplate: Production takes about {{leadTimeLabel}}; sea transit from {{portWithCode}} to {{destPorts}} runs about {{transitDays}} days, for an indicative {{totalDays}} days from order to arrival.
leadTime.dgNote: Class 4.2 dangerous-goods bookings need extra lead time, so book early. The SP 978 weathering window (at least {{sp978Weathering}} days) is part of our production and packing schedule, not added on top of it when the order is planned ahead.
leadTime.indicativeNote: Indicative only — subject to vessel schedule and booking confirmation, and never a guaranteed arrival date.

responsibility.intro: The division of labour for a {{moqLabel}} shipment. We export and document; you and your customs broker clear and deliver at destination.
responsibility.buyerHeader: You and your customs broker handle
factoryItems: Production and per-batch quality control, each batch with a Certificate of Analysis
Dangerous-goods-compliant packing, UN 1361 marking, and the safety data sheet (SDS)
Export documentation — commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin, dangerous-goods declaration, and the SP 978 weathering report
Delivery to {{portFullName}} ({{port}}) under FOB, plus booking support
buyerItems: Ocean freight booking under FOB — or we quote CIF/CFR to your port on request
Import duty, VAT or equivalent taxes, and destination port charges
Final inland delivery to your warehouse
